title: Vouacapoua araroba
taxonomy: Vouacapoua araroba is classified within the family Leguminosae. This family is commonly known as the legume family or pea family. The Leguminosae family is further divided into three subfamilies: Caesalpinioideae, Faboideae, and Mimosoideae. Vouacapoua araroba falls under the subfamily Faboideae. The species Vouacapoua araroba is native to Brazil.

intro: Vouacapoua araroba, also known as Araroba, is a tropical tree species native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the family Fabaceae. The health benefits of Vouacapoua araroba include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Vouacapoua araroba is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often prepared by infusing the bark, leaves, or roots in hot water to release its bioactive compounds.
